question: Which of the following is/are examples of exploratory data analysis?
options: Testing that the average salary in an age group is a specified value
Searching for trends or outliers in a data set
Histogram analysis of runs scored by a player in cricket
Analyzing the relationship between variables using linear regression
explanation: Exploratory data analysis focuses on patterns, distributions, trends, and relationships before formal hypothesis testing.

question: Identify the correct statement/s below.
options: Any deterministic signal can be accurately predicted given sufficient history.
A stochastic signal always keeps randomly alternating between positive and negative values
Measurements of steps by the best smart phone of an active individual are deterministic.
Probability that a batsman in cricket will get out on any ball is 0.5.
explanation: A deterministic signal follows a fixed rule, so past information can predict future values.

question: Which of the following functions is/are linear in time t?
options: A sinusoidal signal y(t)=A sin(ω₀t)
The signal x(t)=t exp(c₀t)+β when c₀=0
Distance travelled by a car with uniform velocity
The instantaneous frequency of a linear chirp x(t)=cos(2π(αt+f₀)t)
explanation: Linear behavior means output changes proportionally with time.

question: An estimator is said to be most efficient when
options: The parameters can be estimated accurately with the smallest sample size.
It estimates parameters with the lowest variability / highest precision.
It is able to handle at least 10% of missing data.
Among all estimators it estimates with lowest standard error.
explanation: Efficiency means minimum variance or standard error among estimators.

question: Identify the correct statement(s) among the following.
options: Bias in an estimate implies it is different from the true value by exactly a constant.
A statistic and estimator are both mathematical functions of the observed data.
Precision and accuracy of an estimator are very closely related to each other.
Two variables with identical correlation in two different data sets have the same linear model.
explanation: Both statistics and estimators are calculated directly from sample data.

question: Identify the correct statement among the following:
options: Data visualization is useful because it gives us an idea of anomalies and trends.
Robust methods of estimation are meant to handle outliers.
Errors in estimates of order 10^-3 are always insignificant.
Input excitation can greatly influence the ability to estimate parameters uniquely.
explanation: Visualization, robustness, and input quality all affect reliable estimation.

question: Which of the following is true concerning a parameter estimate?
options: It can never equal the true value even if data is identical to the ensemble.
Averaging across many data sets reduces error by five hundred times.
Increase in sample size will always increase its value.
The most efficient estimate is not necessarily the most accurate.
explanation: An efficient estimator may still have bias, so accuracy is separate.

question: Which of the following is true with respect to data-driven modelling?
options: Overfitting generally implies more time spent fitting the model.
Models that are underfit are less complex than overfit models.
Overfit models generally perform poorly on fresh data sets.
Overfitting occurs whenever sample size is larger than necessary.
explanation: Overfit models capture noise and fail to generalize.

question: While working with acids in laboratories:
options: Acid storage must be done with secondary containment
Acids and bases must be separated
Acid wastes must be washed down the sink
Acid containing bottles must be clearly labelled
explanation: Safe acid handling requires containment, separation, and labeling.

question: Gas bottles in laboratories should be:
options: Transported by rolling on the floor
Secured with a chain high enough to prevent toppling
Stored with regulators removed
Stored with regulators fixed on the bottles
explanation: Gas cylinders must be secured and regulators removed when stored.

question: An outline of a talk helps the audience
options: Understand your educational background
Note the main points you will be covering in the talk
Decide the sections of the talk they are interested in
Understand the importance of your professional accomplishments
explanation: An outline helps listeners follow structure and focus areas.

question: The features of a journal paper are:
options: It changes based on the audience
It is a one-time activity with respect to a given set of data
It is peer reviewed
Generally interactive with the reader
explanation: Journal papers are formal, fixed documents reviewed by experts.

question: The features of a technical presentation are:
options: It changes based on the audience
It is a one-time activity with respect to a given set of data
It is peer reviewed
Generally interactive with the audience
explanation: Presentations are adapted to audience needs and often interactive.

question: Knowing who your audience is important in a technical presentation because:
options: Your audience may have popular people
You will be able to gauge their background knowledge
You can tailor your talk accordingly
You can understand why they are watching your talk
explanation: Audience awareness improves relevance, depth, and delivery.

question: Illustration and data in a presentation
options: Must completely fill the slide from end to end
Must be presented with sufficient open spaces in between
Must typically help the audience focus on a specific idea in each slide
Must always be in colour
explanation: Slides should remain clear, readable, and idea-focused.

question: Steps involved in publishing a journal paper typically include:
options: Writing the paper as per journal constraints
Submitting the paper to a journal which sends it to reviewers
Responding to reviewer comments
Publication if it is less than 7 pages long
explanation: Publication follows writing, review, revision, and acceptance.

question: Any given journal will typically accept for review:
options: Papers in any area of research
Papers in any area if new
Papers only from a relatively narrow area of research
Previously published peer reviewed papers
explanation: Most journals define a limited subject scope.

question: A technical paper and a science fiction novel:
options: Have the same style of presentation
Have very different styles of presentation
Both aim to surprise the reader in the end
Both aim to be thrilling till the end
explanation: Technical writing prioritizes clarity over narrative suspense.

question: Most students typically find their initial technical papers difficult to write because:
options: They are accustomed to poor examples of technical writing
They have high expectations of the first draft
They often do not understand why changes are suggested
They do not understand their work
explanation: Writing improves through revision and understanding conventions.

question: The purpose of technical writing includes
options: Informing the reader
Persuading the reader to a certain point of view
Conveying ideas efficiently
Being sarcastic in the technical document
explanation: Technical writing must be clear, objective, and purposeful.
